summary: Closed, unmerged PR split out from #636 at reviewer request. The slice mostly contains broad mechanical assembly formatting churn, especially removal of trailing spaces after PowerPC mnemonics like blr/bctrl/blrl, plus a smaller set of decomp-facing cleanup: naming func_803901F0 as GObj_Create, naming Fighter_80068E98 as Fighter_Create, introducing fighter_alloc_data and ftPartsTable, adding fighter part and scene descriptor types, moving HSD_ForeachAnim to the baselib aobj header with a typed prototype, and tightening several baselib/stage/fighter struct declarations.
what_changed: Split the first group of commits from PR #636, explicitly described by the author as done per @ribbanya's recommendation.

Applied large-scale assembly formatting cleanup across 77 files, mostly changing forms like `blr `, `bctr `, and `blrl ` to `blr`, `bctr`, and `blrl` without changing addresses or opcodes in the excerpt.

Renamed the baselib GObj constructor prototype from `func_803901F0(u16 classifier, u8 p_link, u8 priority)` to `GObj_Create(u16 classifier, u8 p_link, u8 priority)` in `include/sysdolphin/baselib/gobj.h`, with callsite updates in C and many asm references.
Renamed fighter creation from `Fighter_80068E98` to `Fighter_Create`, including the helper `Fighter_Create_Inline2` and player allocation callsites in `src/melee/pl/player.c`.
Renamed the fighter object allocator global from `lbl_80458FD0` to `fighter_alloc_data` and updated allocation/free uses in `src/melee/ft/fighter.c` and matching asm references.

Recovered the common-data fighter parts table as `FighterPartsTable** ftPartsTable` instead of `lbl_804D6544`, assigned it from `pData[4]`, exposed it in `fighter.h`, and updated many asm references from `lbl_804D6544@sda21` to `ftPartsTable@sda21`.
Added an `ftPart` enum in `src/melee/ft/fighter.h` with bone/part names such as `TopN`, `TransN`, `HipN`, limb/hand/finger parts, `ThrowN`, and `TransN2`.
Added `FighterPartsTable` in `fighter.h` with `joint_to_part`, `part_to_joint`, and `parts_num` fields, documented as a table in `PlCo.dat`.
Added new `src/melee/sc/scene.h` with `StaticModelDesc`, `DynamicModelDesc`, and `SceneDesc` model/camera/light/fog descriptor structs.
Changed stage header ownership of model animation descriptors by including `melee/sc/scene.h`, removing local `StageAnim`, and replacing `StageInfo.x6CC` with `DynamicModelDesc*`.

Moved `HSD_ForeachAnim` out of the catch-all `include/functions.h` into `include/sysdolphin/baselib/aobj.h` with a typed varargs prototype using `HSD_Type`, `HSD_TypeMask`, and `AObj_Arg_Type`; the asm definition in `aobj.c` was updated to match the typed signature.
Adjusted baselib descriptor typing: `HSD_PObjDesc`, `HSD_EnvelopeDesc`, and `HSD_RObjDesc`/`HSD_RvalueList` joint fields use `struct _HSD_Joint*` instead of `struct _HSD_JObjDesc*`.
smart_moves: Kept broad symbol renames synchronized across C, headers, and assembly, which preserves linkability while making existing asm references more searchable.

Promoted a high-value unknown function to a semantic name, `GObj_Create`, with a clear prototype; this benefits many systems because GObj creation appears throughout camera, effects, fighter, item, menu, and interface code.
Recovered `ftPartsTable` as a typed fighter common-data table instead of leaving it as an anonymous SDA label, making repeated part/joint lookups easier to recognize in future fighter work.
Introduced shared scene/model descriptor structs instead of keeping a stage-only `StageAnim`, reducing duplicated or misleading terminology for data that can describe stages, menus, and HUD overlays.
Moved `HSD_ForeachAnim` to the subsystem header that owns AObj animation traversal, reducing dependence on the global `functions.h` bucket.
Separated this work from the larger PR #636, which was explicitly done to keep review scope smaller.
decomp_lessons: When an unknown function has a clear subsystem role and stable argument pattern, renaming it at the header level and updating asm call labels can improve the whole tree without requiring full decompilation of every caller.
SDA globals that originate from common data tables should be typed and named at the load site; assigning `pData[4]` to `ftPartsTable` documents both origin and layout.
Header cleanup is most useful when it also removes catch-all declarations: moving `HSD_ForeachAnim` from `functions.h` to `aobj.h` makes include boundaries more meaningful.
Shared asset descriptor layouts should live in a neutral header when the same concept spans stages, menus, HUDs, or scenes; avoid stage-specific names for general model/animation descriptors.
Mechanical asm formatting can dominate diffs; future PRs should isolate whitespace-only asm churn from semantic renames and type recovery when possible.
Typed prototypes for varargs functions still matter: naming fixed parameters before `...` documents call ABI expectations and helps future matching attempts.
naming_conventions: `func_803901F0` was renamed to `GObj_Create`, following the subsystem-object verb style used for baselib GObj APIs.
`Fighter_80068E98` was renamed to `Fighter_Create`, using a semantic action name once the function role was clear.
`Fighter_80068E98_Inline2` was renamed to `Fighter_Create_Inline2`, keeping the helper tied to the parent function after the parent rename.
`lbl_80458FD0` became `fighter_alloc_data`, using a descriptive lowercase data-name style for an allocator global.
`lbl_804D6544` became `ftPartsTable`, using the fighter `ft` prefix and a data-structure noun.
`FighterPartsTable` fields use conversion-oriented names, `joint_to_part` and `part_to_joint`, plus `parts_num`.
Scene descriptors use `StaticModelDesc`, `DynamicModelDesc`, and `SceneDesc`, a concise `*Desc` suffix convention for asset-description structs.
The `ftPart` enum uses asset/bone names directly, such as `TopN`, `TransN`, `LLegJA`, `RHandN`, and `ThrowN`, preserving apparent data-file naming rather than forcing C-style enum prefixes.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Assembly labels were updated to call renamed symbols such as `GObj_Create`, `fighter_alloc_data`, and `ftPartsTable` while preserving original instruction addresses and machine-code comments.
Large asm churn appears to be whitespace-only formatting, especially removing trailing spaces after branch-return instructions (`blr`, `bctr`, `blrl`).
Renaming SDA labels in asm comments/references can make cross-references searchable before the containing functions are fully decompiled.

The diff preserves matching-sensitive comments such as the note in `Fighter_Create_Inline2` that using `(void*)` would prevent `Fighter_Create` from matching, indicating care around casts and inline behavior.
A decomp comment warning not to hardcode allocation sizes remains near `HSD_ObjAllocInit`, reinforcing that `sizeof(Fighter)` is preferred where known.
review_feedback: The only explicit review-related evidence is in the PR body: this was split from #636 as the first group of commits per @ribbanya's recommendation.
There were no issue comments, review comments, or submitted reviews in the provided PR slice.
The PR was closed quickly and has no merge timestamp; evidence does not show whether the changes landed elsewhere.
follow_up_queries: Find the later PR or commit where the split-out work from PR #639 was merged or superseded.
Search for `GObj_Create` adoption in later PRs to confirm final naming and prototype stability.
Search for `ftPartsTable` and `FighterPartsTable` follow-up changes to see whether the table layout was refined.
Search for `src/melee/sc/scene.h` later edits to determine whether `StaticModelDesc`, `DynamicModelDesc`, and `SceneDesc` became the canonical scene descriptor types.
Search for PR #636 context to understand the original larger patch and why this subset was split.
Audit later asm cleanup PRs for whether trailing-whitespace-only changes were isolated from semantic decomp work.
